title: Why Enovix (ENVX) Still Has Room to Grow Despite BofA’s Cautious Start
content:
Enovix Corporation (NASDAQ:ENVX) is one of the fastest-growing battery technology stocks to buy.

On March 12, 2026, BofA Securities initiated coverage with a Neutral rating and a $6 price target. The firm said Enovix’s revenue opportunity and large addressable market were being balanced by early manufacturing hurdles, long smartphone qualification periods, and expected negative margins and cash flow as the company continues investing for growth. BofA also described Enovix as a company “leading the charge” in next-generation high-energy-density lithium-ion batteries with ambitions to commercialize a 100% silicon-anode battery.Why Enovix (ENVX) Still Has Room to Grow Despite BofA’s Cautious Start

Photo by Mika Baumeister on Unsplash

That caution sits alongside a business that is still growing quickly from a small base. In late February, Enovix reported record fourth-quarter revenue of $11.3 million and full-year 2025 revenue of $31.8 million, up 38% from 2024. Full-year non-GAAP gross margin improved to 23% from 0.9% a year earlier, helped by higher production volumes and a mix shift toward higher-margin defense batteries. The company ended 2025 with roughly $621 million in cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities, giving it room to keep pushing qualification and commercial scale-up.

Enovix Corporation (NASDAQ:ENVX) develops, manufactures, and commercializes advanced lithium-ion batteries, including proprietary silicon-anode architectures, for smartphones, smart eyewear, defense, industrial, and emerging edge-AI applications.

While we acknowledge the potential of ENVX as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. title: Analysts at BofA Remain Cautious on Enovix (ENVX) Amid Manufacturing Difficulties
content:
Enovix Corporation (NASDAQ:ENVX) is one of the 10 most active penny stocks to buy.Analysts at BofA Remain Cautious on Enovix (ENVX) Amid Manufacturing Difficulties

Analysts are optimistic about Enovix Corporation (NASDAQ:ENVX)’s long-term battery technology potential, despite the risks associated with near-term commercialization.

According to Bank of America analyst Ruplu Bhattacharya, Enovix Corporation (NASDAQ:ENVX) is leading the charge in next-generation high-energy-density lithium-ion batteries. He initiated coverage on March 12, 2026, with a “Neutral” rating and a $6 price target.

The analyst highlighted Enovix Corporation (NASDAQ:ENVX)’s efforts to commercialize a 100% silicon-anode battery capable of providing greater energy capacity in smaller devices such as defense applications, smartphones, and smart eyewear.

However, Enovix Corporation (NASDAQ:ENVX) warns that the potential is limited amid manufacturing difficulties, long qualification cycles for smartphones, and anticipated negative margins and cash flow in the coming years as the company continues to invest in growing production.

The broader sentiment on Wall Street is still positive despite these concerns.

A consensus price target of $12.50 suggests a potential upside of about 146.55%, with nearly 73% of analysts covering the stock maintaining bullish ratings on Enovix Corporation (NASDAQ:ENVX).

Enovix Corporation (NASDAQ:ENVX) is a developer of silicon-anode lithium-ion batteries that utilize a proprietary 3D cell architecture to enhance energy density and cycle life. The company’s primary markets include consumer electronics, defense, electric vehicles, and energy storage.

While we acknowledge the potential of ENVX as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. title: Enovix price target lowered to $25 from $30 at Cantor Fitzgerald
content:
Cantor Fitzgerald lowered the firm’s price target on Enovix (ENVX) to $25 from $30 and keeps an Overweight rating on the shares. Enovix missed the mark in Q3, reporting a cycle life issue with battery samples that led to a roughly one-quarter qualification push-out with its first smartphone customer, Honor, the analyst tells investors in a research note. Enovix is confident that commercial smartphone production will begin in Q2, Cantor notes, adding that the company is progressing nicely with smartphone OEMs and toward mass commercialization.

title: Enovix outlines $9.5M–$10.5M Q4 revenue target while scaling AI-1 battery platform
content:
Earnings Call Insights: Enovix Corporation (ENVX) Q3 2025

MANAGEMENT VIEW

 	* Raj Talluri, President and CEO, emphasized significant advancements in silicon battery technology and strengthened partnership alliances, stating, "Enovix is expanding the limits of battery capabilities and transforming how the battery industry will evolve over the coming years with a silicon battery." He reported 85% year-over-year revenue growth to $8 million, a non-GAAP gross profit of $1.7 million (21% margin), and highlighted the completion of long-term funding bringing total cash and marketable securities to $648 million at quarter-end. 
 	* Talluri noted the AI-1 smartphone battery was validated as "the highest energy density battery reported for a smartphone battery in the industry and... having leading fast charge capabilities." The lead smartphone program with Honor entered final validation, with a 2026 launch planned. He also mentioned progress with a second smartphone OEM and ongoing sampling to other top mobile OEMs.
 	* In smart eyewear, over 1,000 battery packs were delivered for customer qualification, with samples provided to nine additional OEMs and ODMs.
 	* Manufacturing improvements included yield enhancements in Fab2 Malaysia and optimization of the battery formation process. The acquisition of SolarEdge assets was integrated, and the first cell manufacturing capability for 100% active silicon anode technology began in Korea.
 	* New leadership appointments were made, with Dan McCranie joining the Board and Srikanth Kethu heading Enovix India.
 	* CFO Ryan Benton stated, "We completed the [warrant dividend] program at the end of August with all warrants either exercised or expired. Roughly 26.5 million warrants were exercised, generating about $224 million in proceeds, net of fees and expenses... During the third quarter, we repurchased approximately $58 million of common stock."

OUTLOOK

 	* Benton forecasted Q4 2025 revenue of $9.5 million to $10.5 million, "up 25% sequentially at the midpoint."
 	* Non-GAAP loss from operations is projected between $30 million and $33 million, with non-GAAP net loss per share forecasted between $0.16 and $0.20.
 	* Capital expenditures for Q4 are expected to be $9 million to $12 million, primarily for Fab2 equipment and the NPI line in South Korea.
 	* Management stated, "Our guidance does not include mass production for any commercial smartphone shipments to Honor in Q4 2025. Importantly, however, we believe that the customer commitment and launch plans remain firmly intact."
 	* No 2026 guidance was given, though management indicated a "more back-weighted revenue profile next year following end customer qualification and product launches."

FINANCIAL RESULTS

 	* Revenue for Q3 was $8 million, an 85% year-over-year increase.
 	* Non-GAAP gross profit was $1.7 million (21% margin).
 	* Non-GAAP operating expenses were $31.5 million. Non-GAAP loss from operations was $29.8 million.
 	* Adjusted EBITDA improved by $2.3 million year-over-year.
 	* Non-GAAP net loss per share was $0.14.
 	* Cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities totaled $648 million at quarter-end.

Q&A

 	* Mark Shooter, William Blair: Asked about Honor's 1,000 cycle requirement and its impact. CEO Talluri responded, "The requirement has always been 1,000 cycles...we need to make one more small design change to get to the full performance that they want. And we validated that change now internally."
 	* Shooter followed up on the time line for commercialization. Talluri indicated, "It takes 3 to 4 months to validate it after we shipped it...we should expect something in the first half of next year if everything goes well."
 	* George Gianarikas, Canaccord: Sought clarity on production cadence and confidence in design changes. Talluri replied, "I'm very confident...we don't want to launch anything that's not 100% solid, and we work closely with our customers."
 	* Gianarikas inquired about M&A opportunities. Talluri stated, "We're going to do it in a way that is financially makes sense and also does not distract us from our main goal."
 	* Jeffrey Osborne, TD Cowen: Asked about yield improvements in Malaysia. Talluri explained, "Now we've given all the samples to the customers. We are focused on 2 markets, 2 products...the yields are coming up very nicely."
 	* Osborne also queried the nature of the change for Honor. Talluri clarified, "We have to make a chemistry change, not a form factor change, not a scope change."
 	* Colin Rusch, Oppenheimer: Asked about supply chain and material innovation. Talluri described, "We are testing quite a few [silicon anode suppliers]."
 	* Rusch questioned the laptop opportunity. Talluri noted the company is focused on smartphones first but could address laptops quickly after achieving smartphone qualification.
 	* Ananda Baruah, Loop Capital: Inquired about production timelines for both smartphone customers. Talluri confirmed both targeted 2026, with the second customer following a faster process due to knowledge gained with Honor.
 	* Derek Soderberg, Cantor Fitzgerald: Pressed on chemistry change risks. Talluri said, "We have a bunch of different chemistries that we are constantly have as backups...I'm confident that the batteries we shipped this quarter will meet."
 	* Soderberg also asked about breakeven and profitability. Benton explained, "It's as we march towards filling Fab2 with equipment and getting full utilization there that we see as being adjusted EBITDA positive or a proxy for cash flow positive."

SENTIMENT ANALYSIS

 	* Analysts displayed a neutral to slightly positive tone, focusing on technical hurdles, timelines, and production scale-up, while pressing for clarity on commercialization and risk mitigation.
 	* Management maintained a confident and collaborative tone in prepared remarks and Q&A, emphasizing readiness and transparency. Talluri used phrases such as, "I'm very confident...we work closely with our customers."
 	* Compared to the previous quarter, analysts remained focused on commercialization milestones and scaling, with slightly more emphasis on the execution risks in the current call. Management's tone remained confident but addressed more granular technical and production details.

QUARTER-OVER-QUARTER COMPARISON

 	* Revenue increased from $7.5 million in Q2 to $8 million in Q3; non-GAAP gross profit margin declined from 31% to 21% due to product mix and higher costs.
 	* Cash position improved materially, driven by warrant exercises and convertible note issuance.
 	* Guidance for Q4 revenue is higher than Q3’s actuals, with continued investment in scaling and losses in line with prior expectations.
 	* Strategic focus sharpened on smartphone and eyewear markets, with more detailed discussion of manufacturing progress and technical validation hurdles than in Q2.
 	* Analyst questions in Q3 showed increased attention on specific technical milestones and commercialization timing.
 	* Management’s confidence remained strong, but the Q3 call included more details on risk management and contingency planning for technical challenges.

RISKS AND CONCERNS

 	* Management cited the complexity of meeting the 1,000 cycle requirement for Honor and the need for a chemistry change, with Talluri stating, "If this one doesn't work, what do we do next?...we have other backups running."
 	* No mass production for Honor is anticipated in Q4, signaling product qualification risk and potential delays.
 	* Analysts asked about capacity to meet demand, risk of further technical iterations, and breakeven timing.
 	* Benton highlighted continued investment needs and a reliance on customer qualification and product launches for revenue acceleration in 2026.

FINAL TAKEAWAY

Enovix management reports the company is entering a pivotal phase as its AI-1 battery platform achieves industry-leading energy density and progresses through final smartphone and smart eyewear customer validation. With a strengthened financial position and improved manufacturing capabilities, management expresses confidence in meeting customer requirements and scaling toward commercialization in 2026, while acknowledging ongoing technical and qualification risks that are being actively managed with backup chemistries and collaborative customer engagement.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/envx/earnings/transcripts]

title: Enovix (ENVX) Rockets 13% on China Battery Exports Curb
content:
We recently published 10 Big Names Beating Wall Street at its Own Game. Enovix Corp. (NASDAQ:ENVX) is one of the best performers on Monday.

Enovix ended two straight days of losses on Monday, jumping 13.02 percent to finish at $12.67 apiece as investors continued to hunt for shares in firms that are expected to benefit from China’s battery export restrictions.

Investors gobbled up shares in Enovix Corp. (NASDAQ:ENVX), a California-based battery company, on expectations that China’s new export policies would push customers to look elsewhere for supplies.

Additionally, the rally was supported by the US government’s backing of the lithium industry following its recent investment in Lithium Americas, as it aims to prop up domestic manufacturing and reduce reliance on imports.

In other news, Enovix Corp. (NASDAQ:ENVX) last week announced its inclusion in Fast Company’s Next Big Things in Tech list, which highlights emerging technologies such as artificial intelligence and robotics, among others, that have the potential to impact industries.Enovix (ENVX) Rockets 13% on China Battery Exports Curb

Photo by Mika Baumeister on Unsplash

“AI is transforming how we use our devices, but it also puts unprecedented strain on battery performance,” Enovix Corp. (NASDAQ:ENVX) CEO Raj Talluri said.

“Our product architecture enables 100 percent active silicon-anode solutions that deliver the energy density, safety and speed needed for next-generation smartphones and AI devices. Fast Company’s recognition underscores the industry’s need for a step-change in battery performance—and Enovix is delivering it.”

While we acknowledge the potential of ENVX as an investment, our conviction lies in the belief that some AI stocks hold greater promise for delivering higher returns and have limited downside risk. content:
Enovix Corporation (NASDAQ:ENVX) is one of the Best Small-cap EV Stocks to Buy According to Analysts. Enovix Corporation (NASDAQ:ENVX) is set to report its fiscal third-quarter results for 2025 on October 29. Wall Street has a mixed opinion on the stock ahead of the earnings release.

The company posted a revenue of $7.47 million, which grew 98.20% year-over-year, topping estimates by $1.13 million. The EPS of negative $0.22 also stayed in line with the expectations. Analysts have a mixed opinion on Enovix Corporation (NASDAQ:ENVX). For instance, earlier on August 1, Jeff Osborne from TD Cowen assigned a Hold rating to the stock with a $15 price target. However, later on August 28 and September 11, William Blair and Craig-Hallum reiterated a Buy rating on the stock without disclosing price targets, respectively.

More recently, on September 14, Bill Peterson from J.P. Morgan also reiterated a Hold rating on Enovix Corporation (NASDAQ:ENVX) with a $12 price target.

Enovix Corporation (NASDAQ:ENVX) manufactures advanced lithium-ion battery cells that provide higher energy density and storage capacity compared to conventional batteries.

title: Enovix (ENVX) Jumps 11.4% on US Govt Lithium Industry Backing
content:
We recently published 10 Stocks Stealing The Spotlight Today. Enovix Corp. (NASDAQ:ENVX) is one of the top performers on Wednesday.

Shares of Enovix Corp. jumped by 11.43 percent on Wednesday to close at $11.11 apiece as investor sentiment was bolstered by the US government’s support for the local lithium industry.

Enovix Corp. (NASDAQ:ENVX) rallied alongside its counterparts, supported by the US government’s acquisition of a 10 percent stake in Lithium Americas and its subsidiary, in line with plans to boost domestic supply and reduce the country’s reliance on imports.Enovix (ENVX) Jumps 11.4% on US Govt Lithium Industry Backing

Photo by Kumpan Electric on Unsplash

The move sparked expectations that the government could be in the works for new policies to prop up the lithium industry, similar to other industries it considered vital to national security. At present, China is the world’s largest lithium refiner.

While focused on manufacturing lithium-ion batteries, Enovix Corp. (NASDAQ:ENVX) stands to benefit from the Trump administration’s initiatives to ramp up domestic lithium manufacturing, as the move could help lower prices of raw materials for companies like Enovix, which is more appealing than relying on overseas suppliers.

While we acknowledge the potential of ENVX as an investment, our conviction lies in the belief that some AI stocks hold greater promise for delivering higher returns and have limited downside risk.
